published_prior_art.cite: Gunsilius2023DiSCo gives a weighted Wasserstein counterfactual object, not this explicit monotone compatibility-set sharpness statement

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_synth_control_factor_misspec_v1: scalar Chebyshev partial-ID interval under bounded factor misspecification; panel_sdid_mc_id_v1: mean estimator span/equality frontier

published_prior_art.cite: Gunsilius2023DiSCo gives a unique Wasserstein/quantile synthetic-control object inside the DiSCo weighting workflow, not a necessary-and-sufficient rank-invariant finite-donor identification boundary

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_synth_control_factor_misspec_v1: scalar factor-tube partial-ID; no catalogue entry targets distributional rank-factor open-set nonidentification

published_prior_art.cite: Chen2020DistributionalSC and FermanPinto2021ImperfectFit motivate poor/imperfect fit but do not provide a relative-open finite-donor rank-factor nonidentification class
Gunsilius2023DiSCo covers the weighted donor-quantile corner; this restates it as a sufficient stable-loading subcase

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1 / Conjecture 2
novelty_flags.one_line: tier=field below novelty_target=flagship: the strict-extension claim is plausible, but the proposal does not cite a published open-problem statement showing that DiSCo or related distributional-SC work explicitly left the larger rank-invariant quantile-factor class open.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Add a load-bearing quotation or theorem reference from Gunsilius2023DiSCo, Chen2020DistributionalSC, or Masini2025DCA showing the exact assumption class they handle and an explicit limitation/open class that the proposed rank-invariant boundary strictly contains.

Upgrade the flagship claim to a computable frontier between at least two named published workflows, for example DiSCo weighted barycenter versus high-dimensional distributional counterfactual analysis, with an observable augmented row-span threshold and strict non-equivalence off the threshold.

Include a concrete nondegenerate J=3, T0=2 rank-factor witness in the proposal body showing exact pre-period convex fit, augmented row-span failure, and two monotone post-period counterfactual quantile curves.

recommended_next_step: Revise the literature and §8 novelty blocks to establish a flagship-grade strict-extension/open-problem hook, or explicitly pivot the target tier to field.
